name: Banana Bread
description: A cross between banana bread and a drizzle cake — quick to make, freezable, and an excellent use for overripe bananas.
source: Good Food team, BBC Good Food, bbcgoodfood.com

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: 2–3 tsp; for the icing. Not listed in source ingredient list — added from method

steps.title: Prepare the tin
steps.content: Heat oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. Grease a 2lb loaf tin with {0002} and line the base and sides with baking parchment.

steps.title: Cream butter and sugar
steps.content: Cream {0001} and {0003} together until light and fluffy. Slowly add {0004} with a little of the {0005}.

steps.title: Bake
steps.content: Pour the mixture into the prepared tin and bake for about 50 minutes, or until cooked through. Check from around 30–40 minutes in by testing with a skewer — it should insert and remove cleanly. Time may vary depending on the shape of your tin.

steps.title: Cool
steps.content: Cool in the tin for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack.

steps.title: Make the icing
steps.content: Mix {0008} with {0009} to make a runny icing.

steps.title: Ice and decorate
steps.content: Drizzle the icing across the top of the cake and decorate with {0010}.

notes: Cuts into 8–10 slices. Can be frozen un-iced.

_fixture_notes.external_ref: Supplied slug 'banana-bread' used verbatim; not re-derived.
_fixture_notes.base_servings: Source states 'Cuts into 8-10 slices' — direct yield statement. Range convention: base_servings set to 8, range noted in top-level notes.
_fixture_notes.cook_time_minutes: Labelled COOK TIME: 50 mins. Used at face value. Bake step says 'about 50 mins' — consistent. Post-cook rest (Step 5, cool in tin 10 mins) is post-cook rest and excluded per spec. Step 5 timer set to null.
_fixture_notes.prep_time_minutes: Labelled PREP TIME: 15 mins. Used at face value.
_fixture_notes.butter_duplicate: Butter appears as 140g for the batter and 'plus extra for the tin' (greasing). Two distinct uses — separate rows (0001 and 0002) per duplicate-ingredient-across-groups convention. No groups in this recipe so both have group null; distinctness is by use, not group label.
_fixture_notes.water_method_only: 2–3 tsp water appears in Step 6 (icing) but is absent from the source ingredient list. Per the method-only optionals convention, added as a full ingredient row (0009) with amount set to lower bound (2 tsp), range in notes, and source omission noted. Water is not optional for the icing — notes reflect that it was missing from the source list rather than conditional.
_fixture_notes.eggs_prep_modifier: Source lists '2 large eggs, beaten'. 'Beaten' is a prep verb — trailing form already used in source, convention satisfied. 'Large' is a size/selection qualifier, not a prep verb — retained as part of the name.
_fixture_notes.bananas_prep_modifier: Source lists '2 very ripe bananas, mashed'. 'Mashed' is a prep verb — trailing form already used. 'Very ripe' is a selection criterion (canonical-identity modifier), not a prep verb — retained.
_fixture_notes.cuisine: Banana loaf/drizzle cake — neutral British home baking with no strong single-cuisine identity. Left null per cuisine inference convention.
_fixture_notes.dietary_restrictions: Dairy: butter. Eggs: eggs. Gluten: self-raising flour. Baking powder may contain trace starch but self-raising flour is the primary gluten source. No nuts, soy, sesame, shellfish, or peanuts.
_fixture_notes.meal_types: Source describes as a loaf cake/banana bread — assigned snack and dessert. Source does not frame it as a breakfast item despite banana bread commonly serving that purpose; conservative default applied.
_fixture_notes.no_source_notes: Source has no closing notes beyond the freezing tip, which is captured in top-level notes.
_fixture_notes.no_derived_components: Batch mode — no derived components proposed. No obvious reusable component candidates in a simple loaf cake.
